title: Knowledge Is Power

jokes.text: Put a Barney Rubble head on, I'd swear it was Flintstones On Ice.
jokes.setup: Will commenting on Geoffrey's appearance after some physical comedy
jokes.punchline: Comparing Geoffrey to Barney Rubble in an ice show context
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith style pop culture reference with visual comedy element

jokes.text: from looking at the remarkable way your ears protrude from your head.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's formal, polite acknowledgment of being amused
jokes.punchline: Brutal comeback about Will's ears delivered in refined butler style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment - refined delivery of a savage burn with impeccable timing

jokes.text: BWA: Butler With an Attitude.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's unexpected savage comeback
jokes.punchline: Parody of NWA (Niggaz Wit Attitudes) applied to Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture mashup that perfectly captures Geoffrey's unexpected sass - economical and memorable

jokes.text: She totally gave up on Twin Peaks.
jokes.setup: Hillary explaining Toni lacks concentration
jokes.punchline: Using the notoriously confusing TV show as evidence of short attention span
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that shows Hillary's perspective while making a relatable point about the show's complexity

jokes.text: Man, you're looking chiseled.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Uncle Phil lost weight
jokes.punchline: Absurd compliment about Phil being 'chiseled' when he's clearly overweight
jokes.explanation: Classic Will flattery attempt that's transparently false - good character moment but not exceptional

jokes.text: Don't worry. Tonight, we're having ribs.
jokes.setup: Phil's concern about Jazz leaving chicken bones in the car
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting ribs will create an even bigger mess
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the mess concept with economical delivery

jokes.text: I'm going to a world peace benefit hosted by Steven Seagal.
jokes.setup: Hillary wanting to use the car for an evening out
jokes.punchline: Incongruous pairing of world peace with action star Steven Seagal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary moment - vapid celebrity worship mixed with fake social consciousness

jokes.text: Oops, did I say that?
jokes.setup: Ashley innocently mentioning Will's late arrival time
jokes.punchline: Fake-innocent acknowledgment that she just got Will in trouble
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's mischievous side with economical delivery

jokes.text: I'm doing a term paper on owls.
jokes.setup: Will caught being out at 3am on a school night
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous excuse about studying nocturnal birds
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist excuse that actually makes logical sense on its surface - good Will thinking on his feet

jokes.text: Oops again!
jokes.setup: Ashley revealing Will pays her to do dishes
jokes.punchline: Same fake-innocent phrase escalating Will's punishment
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that escalates both the punishment and Ashley's mischief

jokes.text: Oops!
jokes.setup: Ashley revealing Will rents the car to Jazz
jokes.punchline: Third 'oops' moment making Will's punishment a full month
jokes.explanation: Completes the escalating pattern though becoming slightly predictable by the third iteration

jokes.text: I don't know my own strength.
jokes.setup: Ashley has just systematically destroyed Will's month with information
jokes.punchline: Understated comment about her power to cause trouble
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ashley moment - deadpan delivery of her awareness of her own mischievous power

jokes.text: Revenge!
jokes.setup: Will has been systematically destroyed by Ashley
jokes.punchline: Theatrical villain declaration
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main plot with dramatic flair

jokes.text: I saw a lady wearing a sweater so ugly... I was forced to pull over and yell at her.
jokes.setup: Toni apologizing for being late
jokes.punchline: Extreme reaction to fashion offense requiring roadside intervention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation that perfectly establishes Toni's character - the compulsion to pull over and yell is hilariously extreme

jokes.text: Will you cut it out? You always say you've failed... and then you get the test back and you always get a 'D.'
jokes.setup: Hillary claiming she failed her English midterm
jokes.punchline: Toni pointing out Hillary's consistent pattern of getting D's while claiming failure
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about academic self-perception vs reality

jokes.text: You're right. I probably did fine.
jokes.setup: Just being told she consistently gets D's
jokes.punchline: Hillary's oblivious optimism about 'doing fine'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary obliviousness - missing the point that D's aren't 'fine'

jokes.text: This is a job for Sherlock Homeboy.
jokes.setup: Will deciding to investigate Hillary's lies
jokes.punchline: Street-smart variation on Sherlock Holmes
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural mashup that fits Will's character perfectly - street meets classical detective

jokes.text: 1000 hours. Bike not fast enough to keep up with Volvo.
jokes.setup: Will doing detective surveillance
jokes.punchline: Military-style log entry acknowledging bicycle's obvious limitations
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with deadpan delivery of obvious problem

jokes.text: Hi, Willie. - Hi, Willie. - No, just 'Will.'
jokes.setup: Will introducing himself to Toni's roommates
jokes.punchline: Both roommates mishearing his name identically, requiring correction
jokes.explanation: Simple name confusion gag that establishes the roommates as slightly ditzy

jokes.text: Earth to Kimmy.
jokes.setup: Kimmy saying they don't know Hillary, then contradicting herself
jokes.punchline: Cindy's exasperated response to Kimmy's spaciness
jokes.explanation: Common phrase but well-placed for the ditzy roommate dynamic

jokes.text: It's even bigger than I thought.
jokes.setup: Learning Hillary dropped out months ago
jokes.punchline: Will's understated reaction to discovering the scope of the deception
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that shows Will processing the bigger picture

jokes.text: When President Bush said he would never raise taxes... was he lying or just kidding?
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining his debate topic
jokes.punchline: False choice between 'lying' and 'kidding' about a serious campaign promise
jokes.explanation: Sharp political humor with perfect Carlton delivery - the binary choice makes it funnier

jokes.text: Ain't that the truth, Ruth.
jokes.setup: Hillary saying 'time flies when you're studying'
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic rhyming response knowing she's lying
jokes.explanation: Good Will catchphrase usage with underlying sarcasm

jokes.text: What was that technical term again? A quark, right?
jokes.setup: Will pretending Hillary called his head a physics term
jokes.punchline: Using 'quark' (subatomic particle) incorrectly as an insult
jokes.explanation: Clever setup that traps Hillary into pretending scientific knowledge she doesn't have

jokes.text: That was a real scream, Will.
jokes.setup: The awkward physics story exchange
jokes.punchline: Phil's dry, knowing response to the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good Phil moment showing he's not buying the story at all

jokes.text: If you were going to do a research paper... you'd probably have to know something they taught on the first day.
jokes.setup: Will explaining the basic element of physics is matter
jokes.punchline: Pointing out this is elementary knowledge, exposing Hillary's fake story
jokes.explanation: Good logical trap that shows Will's street smarts in exposing the lie

jokes.text: It helps that Toni's roommates have the combined IQ of a raisin.
jokes.setup: Hillary asking how Will found out about her lies
jokes.punchline: Insult comparing the roommates' intelligence to dried fruit
jokes.explanation: Solid insult with good specificity - raisin is an oddly perfect comparison

jokes.text: You're not in any position to tell me what I can or cannot do, Miss Thing.
jokes.setup: Hillary trying to demand Will's silence
jokes.punchline: Will asserting dominance with dismissive 'Miss Thing'
jokes.explanation: Good power dynamic shift with characteristic Will attitude

jokes.text: Carry me.
jokes.setup: Will establishing his power over Hillary
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous demand to be physically carried
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist escalation - economical but completely unexpected and hilarious

jokes.text: Yes, Your Highness?
jokes.setup: Will demanding proper address
jokes.punchline: Hillary forced to use royal titles for Will
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing the power dynamic reversal

jokes.text: of course, his pair of lucky drawers.
jokes.setup: Will listing laundry Hillary must do
jokes.punchline: Making her wash his underwear, called 'lucky drawers'
jokes.explanation: Escalates the humiliation appropriately with characteristically crude Will humor

jokes.text: And from now on, you will pronounce the word 'draws.'
jokes.setup: Hillary protesting washing his underwear
jokes.punchline: Will insisting on his preferred pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific humor - Will asserting his cultural linguistic preference as part of the power play

jokes.text: Do you want me to floss them, too?
jokes.setup: Will's detailed shoe cleaning instructions with toothbrush
jokes.punchline: Hillary's sarcastic suggestion to floss the shoes like teeth
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic escalation that shows Hillary still has some fight left

jokes.text: We are not amused.
jokes.setup: Hillary's sarcastic flossing comment
jokes.punchline: Will using royal 'we' like Queen Victoria's famous phrase
jokes.explanation: Great callback to royal treatment with historical reference - economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Is this the guy you don't want to be standing next to at a party, or what?
jokes.setup: Hillary describing Spider-Man's web-shooting
jokes.punchline: Treating superhero powers as gross bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframe of Spider-Man's powers as social awkwardness - perfectly captures Hillary's superficial perspective

jokes.text: Come on, baby. You doggin' it.
jokes.setup: Will criticizing Hilary's Doctor Octopus voice
jokes.punchline: Street slang critique of her comic book reading performance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Will's perfectionism about comic voices

jokes.text: You have amused us, but His Highness is tired and would like to go to sleep.
jokes.setup: Hillary's improved Doctor Octopus voice
jokes.punchline: Will maintaining royal persona while dismissing her like a servant
jokes.explanation: Good callback to royal treatment maintaining the power dynamic

jokes.text: Not too tight. I wouldn't want you to suffocate.
jokes.setup: Will demanding to be tucked in tight
jokes.punchline: Mock concern about Hilary's well-being while humiliating her
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Will's fake consideration while maintaining control. The threat is implied but present.

jokes.text: I'm very vindictive, I'm just not imaginative.
jokes.setup: Hillary wanting to punish Will but needing Carlton's help
jokes.punchline: Perfect self-aware description of her limitations
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character insight delivered with perfect economy - captures Hillary's personality flaws with brutal self-awareness

jokes.text: Will you clean mine?
jokes.setup: Hillary explaining Will makes her clean his sneakers
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately trying to exploit the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton opportunism - economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Joan Rivers. He's a plastic surgeon.
jokes.setup: Ashley saying Teresa's dad does Joan Rivers
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he's a plastic surgeon who worked on the comedian
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection playing on Joan Rivers' well-known plastic surgery

jokes.text: I bark like a dog.
jokes.setup: Will quizzing Hillary on dinner humiliation tasks
jokes.punchline: Hillary having to bark whenever someone says her name
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humiliation that sets up dinner scene payoff

jokes.text: I smack Will upside the head.
jokes.setup: Carlton reviewing his revenge list
jokes.punchline: Physical violence as dinner entertainment
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup

jokes.text: Damn it, you're prepared! Let's go!
jokes.setup: Carlton testing Hillary on their revenge plan
jokes.punchline: Carlton's enthusiasm about Hillary being ready for revenge
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton moment showing his excitement for the scheme

jokes.text: Will Smith is perfect. Why, thank you. It's so nice to be appreciated.
jokes.setup: Hilary forced to compliment Will at dinner
jokes.punchline: Will's oblivious acceptance of forced praise
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the escalating dinner chaos

jokes.text: Will Smith is the king of the universe.
jokes.setup: Hillary following Will's dinner instructions
jokes.punchline: Absurdly over-the-top compliment interrupting conversation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the dinner humiliation with absurd praise

jokes.text: [Hillary barks like a dog]
jokes.setup: Someone says 'Hillary' at dinner
jokes.punchline: Hillary actually barks like a dog at the dinner table
jokes.explanation: Strong payoff to earlier setup - physical comedy at dinner table is peak awkwardness

jokes.text: Will Smith is the scum of the earth.
jokes.setup: Hillary following Carlton's instruction list
jokes.punchline: Complete opposite insult of Will from Carlton's revenge plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the competing revenge plans in action

jokes.text: However, Will Smith is the pinnacle of manliness.
jokes.setup: Getting mixed signals causing contradiction
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to extreme praise
jokes.explanation: Good confusion escalation as plans conflict

jokes.text: Will Smith is the scum of the earth.
jokes.setup: Carlton's signal triggers Hilary's insult response
jokes.punchline: Complete opposite of her previous compliments
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and contrast. The whiplash from 'king of universe' to 'scum of earth' creates comedic confusion for the parents.

jokes.text: Stop it! I can't take it anymore! I dropped out of school! There!
jokes.setup: Hillary being torn between competing humiliation instructions
jokes.punchline: Breaking down and confessing everything to stop the torture
jokes.explanation: Perfect breaking point moment - good payoff to all the dinner torture

jokes.text: I think it's pretty safe to assume she sang like a canary.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton wondering what Hillary told their parents
jokes.punchline: Carlton using detective metaphor about Hillary revealing everything
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton vocabulary showing his nervousness about being caught

jokes.text: Hope her crazy lies don't drag down anyone else.
jokes.setup: Will trying to distance himself from Hillary's situation
jokes.punchline: Ironically calling Hillary's lies crazy when he was blackmailing her
jokes.explanation: Good Will attempt at self-preservation with characteristic irony

jokes.text: that should pretty much take you into fiscal '91.
jokes.setup: Phil adding a month to Will's existing month grounding
jokes.punchline: Using fiscal year terminology for extreme punishment duration
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phil moment combining his legal background with parental punishment - fiscal year reference is perfect

jokes.text: You mean you bought that crap?
jokes.setup: Vivian expressing sympathy for Hillary's pressure explanation
jokes.punchline: Phil's blunt dismissal of Hillary's manipulation attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil reaction - blunt and cutting through manipulation

jokes.text: You want me to join the Army?
jokes.setup: Vivian saying 'be all you can be'
jokes.punchline: Hillary confusing the motivational phrase with Army recruitment slogan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hillary obliviousness - mixing up inspirational advice with military advertising

jokes.text: You want me to join the Army?
jokes.setup: Parents saying 'be all you can be'
jokes.punchline: Hilary confusing life advice with Army slogan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - completely literal interpretation of common phrase

jokes.text: We're dealing in hypotheticals here.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Carlton would have told on Hillary
jokes.punchline: Carlton's evasive academic-speak to avoid answering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton evasion using formal language to avoid commitment - very characteristic

jokes.text: Does that little placebo make either of you feel any better?
jokes.setup: Carlton and Will claiming they wouldn't have told
jokes.punchline: Carlton using 'placebo' incorrectly but pretentiously
jokes.explanation: Great Carlton malapropism - using medical term incorrectly but with confidence

jokes.text: My lucky 'draws'!
jokes.setup: Hillary finishing Will's laundry as final act of servitude
jokes.punchline: Hillary has clearly done something to Will's underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback payoff to the lucky drawers setup - implies Hillary got final revenge

tmdb_overview: Hilary becomes Will's personal maid when he threatens to disclose she's dropped out of UCLA.
